title: Psychiatric Technicians
description: Care for individuals with mental or emotional conditions or disabilities, following the instructions of physicians or other health practitioners. Monitor patients' physical and emotional well-being and report to medical staff. May participate in rehabilitation and treatment programs, help with personal hygiene, and administer oral or injectable medications.

tasks.text: Provide nursing, psychiatric, or personal care to patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Encourage patients to develop work skills and to participate in social, recreational, or other therapeutic activities that enhance interpersonal skills or develop social relationships.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Restrain violent, potentially violent, or suicidal patients by verbal or physical means as required.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Lead prescribed individual or group therapy sessions as part of specific therapeutic procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor patients' physical and emotional well-being and report unusual behavior or physical ailments to medical staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Take and record measures of patients' physical condition, using devices such as thermometers or blood pressure gauges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe and influence patients' behavior, communicating and interacting with them and teaching, counseling, or befriending them.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Aid patients in performing tasks, such as bathing or keeping beds, clothing, or living areas clean.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with or assist doctors, psychologists, or rehabilitation therapists in working with patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities to treat, rehabilitate, and return patients to the community.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or teach strategies to promote client wellness and independence.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Train or instruct new employees on procedures to follow with psychiatric patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer oral medications or hypodermic injections, following physician's prescriptions and hospital procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Issue medications from dispensary and maintain records in accordance with specified procedures.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Interview new patients to complete admission forms, to assess their mental health status, or to obtain their mental health and treatment history.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Contact patients' relatives to arrange family conferences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
